explanation: The technician should consider user error when troubleshooting an AV system failure because some users do not understand how to properly operate the system. It’s possible that a user has changed a setting or performed another action that caused the failure.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 22, pp. 465-487

prompt: What is the best method to determine whether a microphone is the source of a problem?
choices: Change the cable to the microphone
T est the microphone with a multimeter
Plug the microphone into a preamp and test
Swap out the suspect microphone with a new microphone that you know is working and see whether the system works properly
explanation: The easiest method to test whether a failed microphone is the source of a system problem is to swap out the suspect microphone with a new microphone that you know is working and see whether the system works properly.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 22, pp. 465-487

prompt: Which of the following is not an example of an issue that can cause a video projector to overheat?
explanation: The brightness setting is not a typical reason for a video projector to overheat.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 22, pp. 465-487

prompt: Which of the following is the most likely source of an AV system failure or problem?
explanation: Cables and connectors fail more frequently than components. When cables and connectors are used on a regular basis and not handled carefully, they can be damaged and may present a variety of symptoms.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 22, pp. 465-487

prompt: Which of the following is not a method of addressing feedback within an audio system?
choices: Adding compression to the microphone signal chain
Keeping the microphone as close to the sound source as possible
Keeping the loudspeakers in front of and as far from the microphones as possible
T urning down or muting all unused microphones
explanation: Adding compression to the microphone signal chain will not address feedback. The best practices for controlling feedback include keeping the microphone as close to the sound source as possible, keeping the loudspeakers in front of and as far from the microphones as possible, appropriately positioning the volume (gain), and turning down or muting all unused microphones.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 22, pp. 465-487

prompt: An excessive amount of high-frequency hiss within an audio system is likely due to which of the following situations?
choices: Noise gate thresholds set too low
Source output gain set too high
explanation: Poor gain structure is a typical source of a constant high-frequency hiss in the system. This is because of the inputs on the mixer being set too low and subsequent stages having their gain increased in an attempt to compensate.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 22, pp. 465-487

prompt: Applied scenario 1: A discovery meeting is translating stakeholder goals into functional requirements. Which option is not considered part of active listening?
choices: Focusing on what the person is saying
Summarizing and paraphrasing the person’s statements
Asking frequent questions to guide the conversation to the topic you are interested in discussing
Maintaining eye contact with the person
explanation: Active listening includes focusing on what the person is saying, summarizing and paraphrasing the person’s statements, and maintaining eye contact with a person.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 11, pp. 191-220

prompt: Applied scenario 2: A discovery meeting is translating stakeholder goals into functional requirements. How does knowledge of the overall room function assist the AV system designer in defining the client needs?
choices: Provides a starting point from which to determine client needs
Defines the functionality that the system elements should provide
Provides a standard design that can be used for most clients
Provides a standard design template that can be given to the building architect
explanation: Knowledge of the overall room function assists the AV system designer by providing a starting point from which to determine client needs.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 11, pp. 191-220

choices: Focusing on what the person is saying

Maintaining eye contact with the person

prompt: Applied scenario 8: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. What drawing would you review to find out exactly how the projector should be mounted to the ceiling?
explanation: A detail drawing would depict exactly how the designer wants the projector mounted to the ceiling.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 12, pp. 223-253

prompt: Applied scenario 9: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. Which option equations allows you to convert a measurement of 10 inches into an equivalent measurement in millimeters?
explanation: The equation 25.4 × 10 = 254mm allows you to convert a measurement of 10 inches into an equivalent measurement in millimeters.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 12, pp. 223-253

prompt: Applied scenario 10: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. What are the main concerns of the AV system designer regarding the audience area?
choices: Determine whether the audience can see and hear the presentation, and determine whether movement within the seating area will be comfortable.
The architect addresses the audience area design, not the AV designer.
Determine whether the proposed audience area HVAC and lighting are adequate.
Ensure that the audience seating is placed between 5 feet (1.5 meters) and 25 feet (7.6 meters) from the screen.
explanation: The AV designer should evaluate the overall design to determine whether the audience can see and hear the presentation and that movement within the seating area will be comfortable.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 13, pp. 255-277

prompt: Applied scenario 11: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. A client needs a display system for a room that will be used for inspecting detailed drawings of computer system networks. Which of the online DISCAS tools should be used to make the calculations for screen size, viewer distance, and content size?
explanation: For inspecting detailed images, such as graphics and engineering diagrams, calculations should be made using the online analytical decision-making tool to establish screen size, viewing distances, and the sizes of image content.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 13, pp. 255-277

prompt: Applied scenario 12: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. Which option is not a recommended approach to minimize ambient light levels within a room?
choices: Install lighting controls such as dimmers
T reat windows for light infiltration
explanation: Darker colors are better suited to reduce ambient light levels within a room.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 14, pp. 279-291

prompt: Applied scenario 13: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. Which option is not considered part of the safety equipment that AV technicians should use when working at a work site?
choices: PPE, such as hard hats, work boots, gloves, and safety glasses
explanation: The safety equipment that AV technicians should use when working at a site include PPE (such as hard hats, work boots, gloves, and safety glasses), approved nonconductive ladders, and fall protection.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 12, pp. 223-253

prompt: Applied scenario 14: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. What type of drawing would you use to determine the characteristics of a wall to ascertain the appropriate height and location of a video display?
explanation: Elevation drawings depict the characteristics of a wall using a side view, which would allow you to determine the appropriate height and location of a video display.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 12, pp. 223-253

prompt: Applied scenario 15: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. Which abbreviation, along with a measurement given on a drawing, tells you how high on a wall an interface plate should be installed?
explanation: The abbreviation AFF refers to “above finished floor” and would indicate how high on a wall the interface plate would be installed.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 12, pp. 223-253

prompt: Applied scenario 16: A site team is reconciling drawings with measured field conditions. W hat is the area of a space measuring 15 feet wide by 20 feet long by 10 feet high?
explanation: The area of a space measuring 15 feet wide by 20 feet long by 10 feet high is 300 square feet (length × width).
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 12, pp. 223-253

prompt: Applied scenario 17: A programmer is defining system capabilities, boundaries, assumptions, and cost expectations. Which option cost descriptions provides a general budget for the AV system for use within the AV concept design/program report?
explanation: The opinion of probable cost is a general estimate of the overall cost of the AV system that can be used by the client as a basis for creating a general budget.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 15, pp. 293-311

prompt: Applied scenario 18: A programmer is defining system capabilities, boundaries, assumptions, and cost expectations. Which option identifies the main objective of an AV concept design/program report?
choices: Communicate to the decision-makers about the overall system’s capabilities and budget
Provide a detailed layout of AV components to the general contractor
Provide a listing of specific components to be used within the AV system
Describe the location and layout of AV components within a room
explanation: The concept design/program report provides an overall description of the system, its capabilities, and general cost.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 15, pp. 293-311

prompt: Applied scenario 19: A programmer is defining system capabilities, boundaries, assumptions, and cost expectations. Which option identifies the objective of the program phase of AV system design?
choices: Create the computer software required to control an AV system
Create detailed design documents depicting the AV system components and installation
Describe the AV systems necessary to support the defined needs and the general cost of those systems
Provide a detailed cost quote for the client to approve
explanation: The program phase should focus on defining the user needs, the general AV systems required to meet these needs, and the general costs of these systems.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 15, pp. 293-311

prompt: Applied scenario 20: A programmer is defining system capabilities, boundaries, assumptions, and cost expectations. Which option cost descriptions provides a general budget for the AV system for use within the AV concept design/program report?
explanation: The opinion of probable cost is a general estimate of the overall cost of the AV system that can be used by the client as a basis for creating a general budget.

prompt: Applied scenario 26: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. The amount of ambient light in a displayed environment the missing term or value.
choices: Negatively affects the quality of the displayed image
Does not affect the quality of the displayed image
Improves the quality of the displayed image
Complements the quality of the displayed image
explanation: The amount of ambient light in a displayed environment negatively affects the quality of the displayed image.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 5, pp. 79-121

prompt: Applied scenario 27: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Three of the major formats of digital signal connections are the missing term or value.
explanation: Three of the major formats of digital video connections are DVI, HDMI, and DisplayPort.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 5, pp. 79-121

prompt: Applied scenario 28: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. When selecting a display type, what should be your first step?
choices: Decide if you should use an analog or digital display
Figure out what type of mount you will need
Determine the distance of the farthest viewer
Determine what you want to display
explanation: When selecting a display type, your first step should be to determine what you want to display.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 5, pp. 79-121

prompt: Applied scenario 29: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Which approach should the AV design ensure that the AV system addresses the necessary display signal sources?
choices: By including a signal switcher in the system design
By including all potential signal sources in the AV system control room
By establishing a clear, up-front understanding of the media that the client needs to present
By providing appropriate system inputs for each type of signal source
explanation: During the design stage of the AV system, the designer must have a clear understanding of the client needs, such as the media that the client must be able to present, and.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 16, pp. 313-363

prompt: Applied scenario 30: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Which option identifies the total impedance of a system consisting of three loudspeakers, each with 4 ohms of impedance, connected in series?
explanation: When wiring loudspeakers in a series, the impedance is additive, meaning that three loudspeakers that each have 4 ohms of impedance will result in a system of 12 ohms of impedance.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 16, pp. 313-363

prompt: Applied scenario 31: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Generally, a the missing term or value measurement of incident light is taken at a task area like a video screen.
explanation: Generally, a lux measurement of incident light is taken at a task area like a video screen.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 5, pp. 79-121

prompt: Applied scenario 32: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. According to the Projected Image System Contrast Ratio standard, what is the minimum contrast ratio required for viewing full-motion video?
explanation: According to the Projected Image System Contrast Ratio standard, the minimum contrast ratio required for viewing full-motion video is 80:1.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 5, pp. 79-121

prompt: Applied scenario 33: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Rear-screen display applications are considered the missing term or value.
explanation: Rear-screen display applications are considered transmissive.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 5, pp. 79-121

prompt: Applied scenario 34: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. When designing a video display system, what should be the first consideration of the AV system designer?
choices: How the client is going to use the video display system
The resolution of the video display system
explanation: The primary initial consideration of the AV system designer when designing a video display system component of an AV system is determining how the client will use the display system.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 16, pp. 313-363

prompt: Applied scenario 35: A designer is selecting a technically appropriate solution for the documented need. Which approach should an AV system designer determine the required frequency response of an audio system?
choices: The frequency response should be based on the type of applications the audio is intended to support.
The designer should work to achieve a frequency response as wide as possible within the project budget.
The audio system output frequency response should match the frequency response of the audio source components.
The audio system frequency response should meet the industry standard of 20Hz to 20kHz.
explanation: As with all AV system design decisions, the frequency response of an audio system should be based on the client needs and intended applications.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 16, pp. 313-363

prompt: Applied scenario 36: An integration team is preparing, installing, and verifying the signal path. One of the most efficient configurations for a vertical antenna is to the missing term or value.
choices: Have a physical length one-quarter of the signal wavelength
Have a wavelength one-half the size of the receiving antenna
Have a mounting close to the ground
explanation: One of the most efficient configurations for a vertical antenna is to have a physical length one-quarter of the signal wavelength.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 10, pp. 181-187

prompt: Applied scenario 37: An integration team is preparing, installing, and verifying the signal path. What type of termination does the following picture show?
explanation: The termination depicted in the picture is an example of a BNC connection.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 17, pp. 367-413

prompt: Applied scenario 38: An integration team is preparing, installing, and verifying the signal path. Which option rack-ventilation methods uses a fan that draws air from the rack?
explanation: A ventilation approach that uses a fan to draw hot air from inside the rack is called an evacuation method.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 17, pp. 367-413

prompt: Applied scenario 39: An integration team is preparing, installing, and verifying the signal path. Which option identifies the load limit?
choices: The maximum weight of an equipment rack
The highest intensity a sound system can produce
The weight at which the item will structurally fail
The tendency for an equipment rack to tip over
explanation: The load limit rating refers to the weight at which the item will structurally fail.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 17, pp. 367-413

prompt: Applied scenario 40: An integration team is preparing, installing, and verifying the signal path. Which option objectives should the AV team target when locating microphones for a live event?
choices: Ensure that all microphones are behind loudspeakers
Ensure that all microphone cables are no longer than 15 feet (4.5 meters) in length
Ensure that all microphone cables are taped to the floor
Mount all microphones on nonconductive stands
explanation: The AV team should strive to ensure that all microphones are mounted behind the loudspeakers to minimize any feedback.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 17, pp. 367-413

prompt: Applied scenario 41: An integration team is preparing, installing, and verifying the signal path. The the missing term or value model is a guide that assists with conforming network communications and their processes to standards.
explanation: The OSI reference model is a guide that assists with conforming network communications and their processes to standards.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 6, pp. 123-142

prompt: Applied scenario 50: An integration team is preparing, installing, and verifying the signal path. Which option is a sign of an improperly fabricated cable termination?

prompt: Applied scenario 51: A project lead is coordinating scope, schedule, cost, documentation, and change control. Project budget refers to which of the following?

The total cost of purchasing the needed materials and supplies
The amount of labor and materials the project manager has allocated to complete the project
The amount of labor and materials the project manager used to successfully complete the project
explanation: The project budget is the amount of labor and materials allocated to perform the agreed-upon work.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 19, pp. 429-443

prompt: Applied scenario 52: A project lead is coordinating scope, schedule, cost, documentation, and change control. Which option defines project deliverables and relates the elements of work?

prompt: Applied scenario 53: A project lead is coordinating scope, schedule, cost, documentation, and change control. Which option documents would an AV company submit in the event that a modification in the building design impacted the AV system installation requirements?
explanation: An RFC is the appropriate document for the vendor to submit to the owner in the event that changes to the AV system installation contract are required.

prompt: Applied scenario 54: A project lead is coordinating scope, schedule, cost, documentation, and change control. Which statement best describes the relationship of the AV installation team with other vendors on the site?
choices: The AV team should focus on the AV installation; the project manager is responsible for coordinating work at the project.
The AV team should review the project schedule; it defines all coordination necessary for the project.
The AV team should actively communicate and coordinate with the project manager and other vendors to ensure that the AV installation is not impacted by other vendors working on the project.
The AV team should negotiate work schedules directly with the other vendors.
explanation: The AV team should actively communicate and coordinate with both the project manager and other vendors to ensure that the AV installation is not impacted by other vendors working on the project.
source: Official CTS Exam Guide, 3rd ed., Chapter 19, pp. 429-443

prompt: Applied scenario 55: A project lead is coordinating scope, schedule, cost, documentation, and change control. Which approach should the AV company respond if a client requests changes in the project that end up increasing the cost of completing the project?
choices: Ask the client to agree to a CO that describes the changes to the project agreement and the additional costs.
Perform the requested additional work to ensure that the client is satisfied.
Never perform any work that was not agreed to within the original work contract.
Remove another element of the project to keep the final cost the same.
explanation: The best approach is to document the changes and costs for the changes within a formal CO and have both the client and the company representative sign the CO to confirm that.

A formal process for testing the elements of the AV system to ensure that they operate as intended
